> In order to Grant Arms we will need to establish if

body_html:
Is itpossible to start the design process, before the
granting, I can research my family tree, but the
creative process can start asap

--- Alastair Bruce a.bofc@virgin.net wrote:

Dear Mr Epstein,

Yesterday I spoke with Garter King of Arms, Peter
Gwynn-Jones, about the
plan we have for Granting you Arms. I mentioned
your clear caveat that it
must remain confidential.

Garter is very keen to help you with the Grant,
assuming it falls within his
remit to do so, and feels sure that although the
Grant itself is written "To
all and singular", which you will know is the old
fashioned way of making a
document address everyone, there is no casual
ability for the general public
to access the records at the College of Arms - and
none do.

In order to Grant Arms we will need to establish if
a forebear of yours was
a British subject, or domiciled in the United
Kingdom. If so, as a
descendant, you fall under our remit. If not, we
may have to think again
about how we could do this.

I hope these details will not put you off and, if it
amuses you, please look
at the College of Arms website.

www.college-of-arms.gov.uk

It would be a great pleasure for me to help you and
I think you will enjoy
the process of selecting and designing your own
personal version of the
oldest and most colourful system of personal
identity in western culture.

With very best wishes,

Alastair

-----Original Message-----
From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com]

Subject:
